questions.question: Explain how a force can change the direction of motion of an object.
questions.answer: A force applied to a moving object can alter its direction of motion, such as when a bat hits a moving ball.

questions.question: Why is friction considered both useful and harmful?
questions.answer: Friction is useful because it allows us to walk and hold objects, but it is harmful because it causes wear and tear of surfaces.

questions.question: Explain why objects eventually stop when sliding on the ground.
questions.answer: Objects stop due to friction between the object and the surface, which opposes motion and gradually reduces speed.

questions.question: How does gravitational force affect objects on Earth?
questions.answer: Gravitational force pulls objects toward the center of the Earth, causing them to fall downward.

questions.question: Explain the difference between balanced and unbalanced forces.
questions.answer: Balanced forces are equal forces acting in opposite directions and do not change motion, while unbalanced forces cause a change in motion.

questions.question: Why is friction greater on rough surfaces than on smooth surfaces?
questions.answer: Rough surfaces have more irregularities that interlock with each other, increasing friction.

questions.question: Explain why lubricants are used in machines.
questions.answer: Lubricants reduce friction between moving parts of machines, allowing smoother movement and reducing wear.

questions.question: Why do vehicles have tread patterns on their tires?
questions.answer: Tread patterns increase friction between the tire and the road, improving grip and preventing slipping.

questions.question: Explain how magnetic force acts without contact.
questions.answer: Magnetic force can attract or repel objects such as iron without physical contact because it acts through a magnetic field.

questions.question: Describe how electrostatic force can attract small objects.
questions.answer: Electrostatic force occurs when objects gain electric charge and attract small objects such as pieces of paper.

questions.question: Explain how force can change the speed of an object.
questions.answer: Applying force can increase or decrease the speed of an object, such as accelerating or braking a bicycle.

questions.question: Why does a magnet attract iron objects?
questions.answer: A magnet produces a magnetic field that exerts force on magnetic materials like iron.

questions.question: Explain why it is easier to push a trolley on wheels than drag it.
questions.answer: Rolling friction between wheels and the ground is much smaller than sliding friction, making movement easier.

questions.question: Why does a parachute slow down the fall of a person?
questions.answer: A parachute increases air resistance, which opposes gravitational force and slows the fall.

questions.question: Explain how forces act in tug-of-war.
questions.answer: In tug-of-war, two teams pull in opposite directions, and the team applying greater force wins.

questions.question: Why do objects move when pushed or pulled?
questions.answer: Objects move because an applied force overcomes resistance and changes their state of motion.

questions.question: Explain how air resistance acts as a force.
questions.answer: Air resistance opposes the motion of objects moving through air and slows them down.

questions.question: Why do heavier objects require more force to move?
questions.answer: Heavier objects have greater inertia and require more force to start moving.

questions.question: Explain how forces can change the shape of objects.
questions.answer: Applying force can stretch, compress, or bend objects, changing their shape.

questions.question: Why is friction necessary for walking?
questions.answer: Friction between our feet and the ground prevents slipping and allows us to move forward.

questions.question: Explain how gravity keeps objects on Earth.
questions.answer: Gravity pulls objects toward the center of the Earth, preventing them from floating away.

questions.question: Why do astronauts appear weightless in space?
questions.answer: Astronauts appear weightless because they are in continuous free fall around the Earth.

questions.question: Explain why pushing a wall does not move it.
questions.answer: The wall experiences an equal and opposite force that balances the push, so it does not move.

questions.question: Why is friction reduced by polishing surfaces?
questions.answer: Polishing makes surfaces smoother, reducing irregularities and decreasing friction.

questions.question: Explain why ships can move through water despite friction.
questions.answer: Ships have powerful engines that produce enough force to overcome water resistance and friction.
